revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/87705994|祖神]]"
revision.text:
'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった。
== 主な氏族と祖神の対応 ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、大中臣氏、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など
== References == <references group="注釈"></references> <references group=""></references>
== 参考文献 ==
* <cite id="kotobank">{{Cite web |title=祖神(オヤガミ)とは - コトバンク |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E-677098 |access-date=2019-10-9}}</cite> * <cite id="shinto">{{Citation |title=神道事典 |year=1994 |editor-last=國學院大學日本文化研究所： |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=9784335160332|和書}}</cite><templatestyles src="Module:Citation/CS1/styles.css" /><cite id="shinto">{{Citation |title=神道事典 |year=1994 |editor-last=國學院大學日本文化研究所： |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=9784335160332|和書}}</cite> * <cite id="kodaishi">{{Citation |title=日本古代史大辞典 |year=2006 |editor-last=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元： |publisher=大和書房 |isbn=9784479840657|和書}}</cite><templatestyles src="Module:Citation/CS1/styles.css" /><cite id="kodaishi">{{Citation |title=日本古代史大辞典 |year=2006 |editor-last=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元： |publisher=大和書房 |isbn=9784479840657|和書}}</cite>
== See Also ==
* [[Ujigami|氏神]] * [[Sorei|祖霊]] * [[Veneration of the dead|祖先崇拝]] * [[Euhemerism|エウヘメリズム]] * [[Uji (clan)|氏#古代氏族としての「氏」]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Goryō faith]] [[Category:Ghosts]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto]]

'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、an ancestral deity as related to an [[ujigami]]
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref>『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== 主な氏族と祖神の対応 ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、大中臣氏、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref>『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== 主な氏族と祖神の対応 ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、大中臣氏、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、an ancestral deity as related to an [[ujigami]]

本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。

* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、大中臣氏、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、an ancestral deity as related to an [[ujigami]]
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref>『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。

revision.text:
'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、an ancestral deity as related to an [[ujigami]]
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref>『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== Correspondence between major clans and their ancestral deities ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House]]- [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], [[Hachiman|Hachiman God]], etc. * [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], Ohnakaomi clan, [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], Urabe clan (Izu and Iki lineage)- [[Ame-no-Koyane|Amemori-Koyanomikoto]] etc. * Izumo clan, [[Haji clan|Doji clan]], [[Sugawara clan|Sugawara clan]]-[[Ame no Hohi|Amenohonimikoto]], Takehiratomikoto, Kushiyatamakami, [[Nomi no Sukune|Nomi Shukune]], etc. * [[Musashi kokuzo]], other eastern kokuzo, etc. - Takehiratonomikoto, [[Isetsuhiko]], etc. * [[Mononobe clan]], Hozumi clan - [[Nigihayahi|Niohayahi]], Umashimasatomikoto, etc. * [[Imbe clan|Imobe clan]] (Saibe clan) - [[Futodama|Amantama-no-Mikoto]], Amahiritono-Mikoto, Amatomi-Mikoto, Amahitowashi-Kami, and other [[Imbe clan|Imbe five gods]] * [[Soga clan|Soga clan]], [[Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], Gose clan, Ki clan (Ki Asaomi), etc.- [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takeuchi Shukune]], etc. * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo clan]], Saeki clan - [[Ama-ninichimikoto]], etc. * [[Owari clan, Tsumori clan, Amabe clan-[[Amenohoakari|Amenohimei no Mikoto]], etc. * [[Azumi people|Agushi clan]]- [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi no Mikoto]], Utsushihikanejinjinomikoto, etc. * [[Suwa clan|Suwa clan]]-[[Takeminakata|Kamiminakata]], Yasaka Tosasami, etc. * [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]-[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Kenkakushi no Mikoto]], etc. * [[Kibi clan]-[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]], Wakamatsuhiko-no-mikoto]], etc. * [[Abe clan]-[[Oyohiko-no-Mikoto]], Wakamatsuhiko-no-Mikoto], etc. * [[Miwa clan|Ogami clan]]- [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]], Otadaneko, etc. * [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan (geigi)]] (Kamo-kun, Kamo Asaomi) - [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-kami]], [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]], etc. * The Mono clan (Kami-Mono Kunizo and Shimo-Mono Kunizo) - Toyoki-Irihiko-mikoto, etc. * Aso clan - Kenbanryu-mikoto, Kamihachiimikoto, etc. * The Tata clan (Kano kunizo and Inba kunizo) - Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto, Kami Yaoimimi no Mikoto, etc. * The Usa clan - Utsusatsuhiko, etc. * Ki clan (Kii-no-kunizo) - Amadone-no-Mikoto, etc. * The Yi, Kasuga, Ono, Jobe, etc. - Amadashihikonokuni-Oshihito-mikoto, etc. * [[Chichibu Province|Chichibu Kunizou]]-[[Omoikane (Shinto)|Hachimikane]], Amagasharu-no-Mikoto, etc. * [[Yamato-no-kunizo (Yamato clan), Oyato clan, Yamato clan]]-[[Kubiki kunizo]]- Shiinetsuhiko, etc. * The Zen Clan - Banjuroku T.Karino, etc. * Yamato-jun - Ama-no-tsutsuyujin, etc.
Translated with www.DeepL.com/Translator (free version)

revision.comment: /* Correspondence between major clans and their ancestral deities */
revision.text:
'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、an ancestral deity as related to an [[ujigami]]
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref>『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== Correspondence between major clans and their ancestral deities == {{Main|Draft:Oyagami list}} * [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House]]- [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], [[Hachiman|Hachiman God]], etc. * [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], Ohnakaomi clan, [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], Urabe clan (Izu and Iki lineage)- [[Ame-no-Koyane|Amemori-Koyanomikoto]] etc. * Izumo clan, [[Haji clan|Doji clan]], [[Sugawara clan|Sugawara clan]]-[[Ame no Hohi|Amenohonimikoto]], Takehiratomikoto, Kushiyatamakami, [[Nomi no Sukune|Nomi Shukune]], etc. * [[Musashi kokuzo]], other eastern kokuzo, etc. - Takehiratonomikoto, [[Isetsuhiko]], etc. * [[Mononobe clan]], Hozumi clan - [[Nigihayahi|Niohayahi]], Umashimasatomikoto, etc. * [[Imbe clan|Imobe clan]] (Saibe clan) - [[Futodama|Amantama-no-Mikoto]], Amahiritono-Mikoto, Amatomi-Mikoto, Amahitowashi-Kami, and other [[Imbe clan|Imbe five gods]] * [[Soga clan|Soga clan]], [[Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], Gose clan, Ki clan (Ki Asaomi), etc.- [[Takenouchi no Sukune|Takeuchi Shukune]], etc. * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo clan]], Saeki clan - [[Ama-ninichimikoto]], etc. * [[Owari clan, Tsumori clan, Amabe clan-[[Amenohoakari|Amenohimei no Mikoto]], etc. * [[Azumi people|Agushi clan]]- [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi no Mikoto]], Utsushihikanejinjinomikoto, etc. * [[Suwa clan|Suwa clan]]-[[Takeminakata|Kamiminakata]], Yasaka Tosasami, etc. * [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]-[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Kenkakushi no Mikoto]], etc. * [[Kibi clan]-[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]], Wakamatsuhiko-no-mikoto]], etc. * [[Abe clan]-[[Oyohiko-no-Mikoto]], Wakamatsuhiko-no-Mikoto], etc. * [[Miwa clan|Ogami clan]]- [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]], Otadaneko, etc. * [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan (geigi)]] (Kamo-kun, Kamo Asaomi) - [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-kami]], [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]], etc. * The Mono clan (Kami-Mono Kunizo and Shimo-Mono Kunizo) - Toyoki-Irihiko-mikoto, etc. * Aso clan - Kenbanryu-mikoto, Kamihachiimikoto, etc. * The Tata clan (Kano kunizo and Inba kunizo) - Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto, Kami Yaoimimi no Mikoto, etc. * The Usa clan - Utsusatsuhiko, etc. * Ki clan (Kii-no-kunizo) - Amadone-no-Mikoto, etc. * The Yi, Kasuga, Ono, Jobe, etc. - Amadashihikonokuni-Oshihito-mikoto, etc. * [[Chichibu Province|Chichibu Kunizou]]-[[Omoikane (Shinto)|Hachimikane]], Amagasharu-no-Mikoto, etc. * [[Yamato-no-kunizo (Yamato clan), Oyato clan, Yamato clan]]-[[Kubiki kunizo]]- Shiinetsuhiko, etc. * The Zen Clan - Banjuroku T.Karino, etc. * Yamato-jun - Ama-no-tsutsuyujin, etc.

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/87705994|祖神]]"
revision.text:
'''祖神'''（Oyagami、Sojin、Soshin）refers to、
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref> 『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== 主な氏族と祖神の対応 ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref> 『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== 主な氏族と祖神の対応 ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、卜部氏（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} refers to、
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== 概要 == 主に、[[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]および神別氏族、また上古の早い段階に皇室から分かれた皇別氏族において、祖先に当たる[[Age of the Gods|神代]]の神、あるいは上古の人物を「祖神」とする。「'''[[Uji (clan)|氏]]'''」とは'''共同の祖神'''をもつ'''同族意識によって結合した生活共同体'''であり、本来は「[[Ujigami|氏神]]」と同義であったが、その実態が歴史的に薄れて、'''中世以降'''は氏神は後に血縁から地縁へと変化し、地域の[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]・'''[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]'''と同様の神へと変化していった<ref> 『[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 ブリタニカ国際大百科事典]』「氏神」の頁。</ref>。
== List of Oyagamis ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、[[卜部氏]]（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} refers to、
# ある'''[[Clan|氏族]]'''において、その[[Ancestor|先祖]]とされる[[Deity|神]]、あるいは神として祀った先祖の[[Soul|霊]]（[[Sorei|祖霊]]）<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜デジタル大辞泉｜大辞林 第三版]]</ref>。 # （おやがみ）個人の守り神。[[Sado|佐渡]]では[[Chinjugami|鎮守神]]のことを指す<ref name="kokugo"> [[祖神#kotobank|コトバンク｜精選版 日本国語大辞典]]</ref>。[[Dōsojin|道祖神]]は「どうそじん、どうそしん」と読むが、この概念に近い。 # （そじん・そしん）ある業種において、その始祖とされる神、あるいは[[Tutelary deity|守護神]]。例：薬祖神、料理祖神
本項では主に 1. について、ことに[[Shinto|神道]]の[[Kami|神]]について記す。
== Overview == In the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and the Uji clans, as well as in the Uji clans that split off from the Imperial Household early in the Joko era, the ancestral god of the [[Age of the Gods]], or a person from the Joko era, is considered the '''ancestor god'''. The ''''[[Uji (clan) | clan]]'''' is a ''''community of life united by a sense of kinship''' with a '''common ancestor god'''' and was originally synonymous with ''[[Ujigami|Ujigami]],''' but its reality has faded historically and '''since the Middle Ages''' the clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties. and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami|Usunagami]] and '''[[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]'''<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.
== List of Oyagamis ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、[[卜部氏]]（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * 阿倍氏（安倍氏）－大彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * 毛野氏（上毛野国造・下毛野国造）－豊木入日子命など * 阿蘇氏－健磐龍命、神八井耳命など * 多氏、科野国造、印波国造－神八井耳命など * 宇佐氏－菟狭津彦など * 紀氏（紀伊国造）－天道根命など * 和珥氏、春日氏、小野氏、丈部氏など－天足彦国押人命など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など * 倭国造（倭氏・大倭氏・大和氏）、久比岐国造－椎根津彦など * 膳氏－磐鹿六鴈など * 倭文氏－天羽槌雄神など

The ''''[[Uji (clan) | clan]]'''' is a ''''community of life united by a sense of kinship''' with a '''common ancestor god'''' and was originally synonymous with ''[[Ujigami|Ujigami]],''' but its reality has faded historically and '''since the Middle Ages''' the clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties. and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami|Usunagami]] and '''[[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]'''<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.
== List of Oyagamis ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、[[卜部氏]]（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など

{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} refers to、
# [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain ''''[[Clan|clan]]''''<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> [[Ancestor# kotobank|kotobank|Digital Daijisen|Daijirin 3rd ed.]]</ref> # (Oyagami) A personal guardian deity. In [[Sado|Sado]], it refers to [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref name="kokugo"> [[Oyagami#kotobank|Kotobank｜Elaboratory of the Japanese Language, Selected Edition]]</ref>. [[Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] is read as "Dōsojin, Dōso-shin", which is close to this concept. # (Sōjin, Sōshin) In a certain type of business, the deity considered to be its founder or [[Tutelary deity|Guardian deity]]. Examples: ancestor god of medicine, ancestor god of cooking.
In this section, we will mainly discuss 1. and especially [[Kami|gami]] in [[Shintoism]].

== Overview == In the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and the Uji clans, as well as in the Uji clans that split off from the Imperial Household early in the Joko era, the ancestral god of the [[Age of the Gods]], or a person from the Joko era, is considered the '''ancestor god'''. The ''''[[Uji (clan) | clan]]'''' is a ''''community of life united by a sense of kinship''' with a '''common ancestor god'''' and was originally synonymous with ''[[Ujigami|Ujigami]],''' but its reality has faded historically and '''since the Middle Ages''' the clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties. and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami|Usunagami]] and '''[[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]'''<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.

# [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain '[[Clan|clan]]'<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> {{Cite web |last=字通,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,普及版 |title=祖神(そしん)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E-677098 |access-date=2023-02-05 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> # (Oyagami) A personal guardian deity. In [[Sado|Sado]], it refers to [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref name="daijisen-daijirin" />. [[Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] is read as "Dōsojin, Dōso-shin", which is close to this concept. # (Sōjin, Sōshin) In a certain type of business, the deity considered to be its founder or [[Tutelary deity|Guardian deity]]. Examples: ancestor god of medicine, ancestor god of cooking.
In this section, we will mainly discuss 1. and especially [[Kami]] in [[Shintoism]]. == Overview == In the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and the Uji clans, as well as in the Uji clans that split off from the Imperial Household early in the Joko era, the ancestral god of the [[Age of the Gods]], or a person from the Joko era, is considered the ancestor god. The [[Uji (clan) |clan]] is a community of life united by a sense of kinship with a common ancestor god and was originally synonymous with [[Ujigami|Ujigami]], but its reality has faded historically and since the Middle Ages. The clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami|Usunagami]] and [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.
== List of Oyagamis ==
* [[Imperial House of Japan|皇室]]－[[Amaterasu|天照大御神]]、[[Hachiman|八幡神]]など * [[Nakatomi clan|中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[Fujiwara clan|藤原氏]]、[[卜部氏]]（伊豆系・壱岐系）－[[Ame-no-Koyane|天児屋命]]など * 出雲氏、[[Haji clan|土師氏]]、[[Sugawara clan|菅原氏]]－[[Ame no Hohi|天穂日命]]、建比良鳥命、櫛八玉神、[[Nomi no Sukune|野見宿禰]]など * 武蔵国造、その他東国諸国造など－建比良鳥命、[[Isetsuhiko|伊勢津彦]]など * [[Mononobe clan|物部氏]]、穂積氏－[[Nigihayahi|饒速日命]]、宇摩志麻遅命など * [[Imbe clan|忌部氏]]（斎部氏）－[[Futodama|天太玉命]]、天比理刀咩命、天富命、天日鷲神ら[[Imbe clan|忌部五部神]] * [[Soga clan|蘇我氏]]、[[Katsuragi clan|葛城氏]]、巨勢氏、紀氏（紀朝臣）など－[[Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰]]など * [[Ōtomo clan (ancient)|大伴氏]]、佐伯氏－天忍日命など * 尾張氏、津守氏、海部（あまべ）氏－[[Amenohoakari|天火明命]]など * [[Azumi people|阿曇氏]]－[[Watatsumi|綿津見神]]、宇都志日金拆命など * [[Suwa clan|諏訪氏]]－[[Takeminakata|建御名方神]]、八坂刀売神など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂県主氏]]－[[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|賀茂建角身命]]など * 吉備氏－[[Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|吉備津彦命]]、稚武彦命など * [[Miwa clan|大神氏]]（三輪氏・大三輪氏・神氏）－[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]、大田田根子など * [[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（地祇）]]（鴨君・賀茂朝臣）－[[Ajisukitakahikone|味耜高彦根命]]、[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]など * [[Chichibu Province|知々夫国造]]－[[Omoikane (Shinto)|八意思兼神]]、天下春命など
== References == <references group="注釈"></references> <references group=""></references>
== 参考文献 ==

{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} refers to、
# [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain '[[Clan|clan]]'<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> {{Cite web |last=字通,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,普及版 |title=祖神(そしん)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E-677098 |access-date=2023-02-05 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> # (Oyagami) A personal guardian deity. In [[Sado|Sado]], it refers to [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref name="daijisen-daijirin" />. [[Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] is read as "Dōsojin, Dōso-shin", which is close to this concept. # (Sōjin, Sōshin) In a certain type of business, the deity considered to be its founder or [[Tutelary deity|Guardian deity]]. Examples: ancestor god of medicine, ancestor god of cooking.
In this section, we will mainly discuss 1. and especially [[Kami]] in [[Shintoism]]. == Overview == In the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and the Uji clans, as well as in the Uji clans that split off from the Imperial Household early in the Joko era, the ancestral god of the [[Age of the Gods]], or a person from the Joko era, is considered the ancestor god. The [[Uji (clan) |clan]] is a community of life united by a sense of kinship with a common ancestor god and was originally synonymous with [[Ujigami|Ujigami]], but its reality has faded historically and since the Middle Ages. The clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami|Usunagami]] and [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.

{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain '[[clan]]'<ref name="daijisen-daijirin"> {{Cite web |last=字通,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,普及版 |title=祖神(そしん)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E-677098 |access-date=2023-02-05 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref>'''2006''', , Third Edition (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Sanseidō]], →ISBN 4-385-13905-9</ref><ref>https://archive.ph/rh4cM#selection-2855.0-2889.50</ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/6Edss</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo |date=1954 |publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters |edition=Tenth, 2006 |location=Tenri, Nara, Japan |page=3}}</ref><ref>https://archive.ph/6Edss</ref>
== Overview == In the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and the Uji clans, as well as in the Uji clans that split off from the Imperial Household early in the Joko era, the ancestral god of the [[Age of the Gods]], or a person from the Joko era, is considered the ancestor god. The [[Uji (clan) |clan]] is a community of life united by a sense of kinship with a common ancestor god and was originally synonymous with [[Ujigami|Ujigami]], but its reality has faded historically and since the Middle Ages. The clan gods later shifted from blood to land ties and changed to a deity similar to the local [[Ubusunagami]] and [[Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>.
== See Also == * [[Ujigami]] * [[Sorei]] * [[Veneration of the dead]] * [[Euhemerism]] * [[Uji (clan)|Uji]]

{{Short description|Shinto deity}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|soc}} {{AfC submission|||ts=20230409205300|u=Immanuelle|ns=118}} {{Draft}}
{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}} is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>https://archive.ph/rh4cM#selection-2855.0-2889.50</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">https://archive.ph/wip/6Edss</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
== Other pages ==
* {{ill|Ujigami|en}} * {{Ill|Sorei|en}} * {{Ill|Veneration of the dead|en}} * {{Ill|Euhemerism|en}} * {{Ill|Uji|en}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Type of Shinto deity}}
'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

{{Short description|Type of Shinto deity}}
'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

{{Short description|Type of Shinto deity}}
'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />
== Ancestral deities ==
The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

{{Short description|Type of Shinto deity}}
'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[Ancestor]], [[Deity]], or [[Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />
Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami
In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />

The [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>
== Other uses == The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>
It is the title [[God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />

Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami

Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />

The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>

'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an [[:en:Ancestor|Ancestor]], [[Kami|Deity]], or [[:en:Soul|Soul]] ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain [[:en:Clan|Clan]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />

Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami

In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />

== Ancestral deities ==

The [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>

This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />

Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami

In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />

== Ancestral deities ==

The [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Household]] and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>

== Other uses ==
The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>

It is the title [[:en:God|God]] goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

{{Short description|Type of Shinto deity}}

'''{{Nihongo|Oyagami|祖神|4=Soshin, Sojin}}''' is a term in Shinto that refers to an {{ill|Ancestor|en|Ancestor|WD=Q402152|lt=Ancestor|1=Ancestor}}, [[Kami|Deity]], or {{ill|Soul|en|Soul|WD=Q9165|lt=Soul|1=Soul}} ([[Sorei]]) of an ancestor who was worshipped as a deity in a certain {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}}<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-01-14 |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |access-date=2023-08-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120114065811/https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html |archive-date=2012-01-14 |url-status=live }}</ref> When not used to express the idea of a tutelary deity, it is used to express a connotation of kami caring for human beings in the same way that human parents care for their children. This can be seen as analogous to the phrase [[W:God the Father|God the father]].<ref name=":0" />

Some Shinto sects believe that the entire world or cosmos is a living entity, full of vitality and productive power that originates from the oyagami, which is the ultimate existence that gives birth to everything. According to this belief, humans are "offspring of the kami" and have been given life by them..<ref name=":0" /> This belief is linked to the deity [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] or the first kami

In the Tenrikyō new religion, the main deity is called "Oyagami," and the sect's founder is known as "Oyasama." This use of the word "parent" before the name of the kami being worshipped is believed to reflect the traditional Japanese understanding of the meaning of divine beings. Overall, the concept of oyagami emphasizes the close relationship between humans and kami and the belief that divine beings care for humans like parents care for their children.<ref name=":0" />

== Ancestral deities ==

The {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} and many other [[Japanese clans]] believe in Oyagami as gods they are descended from, also known as [[Ujigami]]. They are considered their original ancestor to be the Oyagami. The notion of the Oyagami as a common ancestor helped create unity, as [[Ujigami]] also did prior to their transition from ancestral deities to land linked deities. The clan gods used to be based on family ties, but later became more connected to the land they lived on, and were like other local gods.<ref>"[https://kotobank.jp/word/氏神-34471 International Encyclopedia Britannica], "Ujigami", p. </ref>

== Other uses ==
The word means "parent god".<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Oyagami |url=https://archive.today/20230316223027/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9710 |access-date=2023-08-31 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref>

It is the title {{ill|God|en|God|WD=Q190|lt=God|1=God}} goes by in [[Tenrikyo]].<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Doctrine of Tenrikyo|date=1954|publisher=Tenrikyo Church Headquarters|edition=Tenth, 2006|location=Tenri, Nara, Japan|page=3}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
